terms.definition: A sweet, sugary liquid produced by flowers to attract bees and other insects for pollination.
terms.exampleUsage: The bee drank nectar from the sunflower and flew back to the hive.

terms.definition: A fine yellow powder found inside flowers. It needs to be carried to other flowers for plants to make seeds and fruits.
terms.exampleUsage: Pollen stuck to the bee's legs as it landed on the flower.

terms.definition: The process of carrying pollen from one flower to another, helping plants produce fruits and seeds. Bees are important pollinators.
terms.exampleUsage: Without pollination by bees, many fruits would not grow.

terms.definition: The home of a honeybee colony, made of wax. It contains thousands of hexagonal cells for storing honey and raising baby bees.
terms.exampleUsage: The beekeeper carefully opened the beehive to collect honey.

terms.definition: A structure of tiny six-sided wax cells inside a beehive where honey is stored and baby bees grow.
terms.exampleUsage: The honeycomb was full of golden honey ready to be harvested.

terms.definition: The single female bee in a hive that lays all the eggs. She is the largest bee and mother of all the bees in the colony.
terms.exampleUsage: The queen bee can lay up to 2,000 eggs in one day.

terms.definition: Female bees that do all the work in the hive - collecting nectar, making honey, feeding babies, guarding the hive, and cleaning.
terms.exampleUsage: Worker bees fly from flower to flower collecting nectar all day.

terms.definition: The practice of keeping bees in special boxes to collect honey and beeswax. Also called beekeeping.
terms.exampleUsage: Many farmers in India practise apiculture to earn extra income.

terms.definition: A natural wax produced by bees to build honeycomb cells. Used by people to make candles, lip balms, and polish.
terms.exampleUsage: The candle was made from natural beeswax collected from the hive.

terms.definition: A large group of bees that live and work together in a single hive. A colony can have 20,000 to 60,000 bees.
terms.exampleUsage: The entire colony works together to protect the queen and make honey.
